params.name: Einthoven's ECG
params.description:
On Nov 12, 1901 AD, the Dutch physiologist Einthoven obtained a clear and stable electrocardiogram by improving the string-type galvanometer.
This electrocardiogram was personally recorded by Dr. Einthoven. Known as the father of the electrocardiogram, he not only independently designed a new type of string-type galvanometer but also used this instrument to expand the electrocardiogram that originally only included two ventricular waves to include six sets of waveforms version and is still in …
